title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, drafted first overall by the Mavericks, played his first NBA game at Boston's TD Garden.
Flagg, a Maine native and Celtics fan, expressed commitment to the Mavericks, stating, 'I love being a Maverick.'
Flagg averaged 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds as a rookie; his contract keeps him with Dallas through at least 2029.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after a 42-and-a-half week absence, scoring 15 points against the Mavericks.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's approach despite early shooting struggles, highlighting his rebounding and defensive contributions.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference and Tatum's return boosts their lineup ahead of the upcoming playoffs.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics with no minutes restriction after a 10-month recovery from a torn Achilles.
Tatum scored 15 points with 12 rebounds and seven assists, contributing to a 120-100 win over the Mavericks.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 24 points as Nikola Vucevic sustained a finger injury, sidelining him for a month.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins GM Don Sweeney chose not to make major trade moves, focusing on long-term rebuilding efforts.
The Bruins traded minor league assets but avoided high-price rentals, preserving future draft picks and prospects.
Sweeney emphasized commitment to the team’s growth, maintaining a strategic approach for sustained success.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby will be traded from the Las Vegas Raiders to the Baltimore Ravens for two first-round draft picks.
Despite Crosby's interest in the Patriots, New England did not match Baltimore's offer for the star defensive end.
New England has over $60 million in cap space for trades and signings, following a reported failed offer for A.J. Brown.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic fractured his right ring finger against the Mavericks, sidelining him for approximately one month.
Vucevic suffered the injury during the first half, which limited him to playing only two minutes without scoring.
His absence impacts Celtics' front-court depth, relying more on players like Neemias Queta and Luka Garza.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics lineup against the Mavericks after recovering from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum played six minutes in his first game back, with two assists, two rebounds, and a dunk and three-pointer.
The Celtics, despite roster changes and injuries, are second in the Eastern Conference with a record of 41-21.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, reports ESPN.
The trade, pending league approval, addresses Bears' need after Drew Dalman's retirement.
Patriots' trade reportedly frees up $5.7 million in cap space, potentially impacting their free agency plans.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Weisz stars in 'Vladimir,' a new Netflix drama about academic scandals and personal turmoil on college campuses.
'Hamnet,' streaming on Peacock, offers a fictional take on Shakespeare’s personal tragedies with Jessie Buckley and Paul Mescal.
Hulu features Jafar Panahi's politically charged film, 'It Was Just an Accident,' exploring past Iranian political prisoners.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics' starting lineup, replacing Baylor Scheierman, after nearly 10 months of injury recovery.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's commitment, highlighting his vulnerability and communication throughout the rehabilitation process.
The Celtics face the Mavericks at TD Garden with Tatum's comeback celebrated amid a focus on winning the game.
